result: One shared ExecDepsOptions contract is recommended for scope.run and ctx.exec: name, deps, and params required; fn receives inferred deps then params and never ctx or scope; core factories and extension observation retain context access.

wall_gate.reason: The exact-head contract checker and independent validator replay do not yet exist.

next_check_at: Independent validator dispatch within the frame freshness window.
commanded_lines: Action envelope: read exact-head Lite public types, runtime, extensions, tests, lint, docs, history, and call candidates; write only workers/dkr-a-exec-api/checkpoint.v1.json and progress.finish.json; no product, git, frame, tree, ledger, release, or external-state changes.

API decision: share one ExecDepsOptions between scope.run and ctx.exec; require name, deps, and params; use deps: {} and params: [] for the empty form; callback receives deps then params and never ctx or scope.
Boundary decision: core may own and use ExecutionContext internally; atom, flow, resource, and preset factories keep lifecycle contexts; Extension.wrapExec keeps ctx for observation and policy.
Compatibility: remove RunFnOptions and ExecFnOptions context-taking inline callbacks; migrate stale docs and the broad source candidates; exact count remains checker-owned.
Anti-goal coverage: all 8 packet walls are carried in checkpoint.v1.json; breached or unverified reads block downstream advance.
Introspection cadence: checkpoint acceptance, any flag, level descent, terminalization, and every third check-in.
Standing walls remain standing and retire only through human-owned frame or standing-wall retirement.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
